message:
perf(ffs-cli): replace full-image reads with seeked file-region I/O

The CLI commands for dumping superblocks, group descriptors, and inodes
previously loaded the entire filesystem image into memory via
\`std::fs::read()\`. For multi-gigabyte images this caused excessive
memory usage and slow startup, even when only a few hundred bytes of
metadata were needed.

This commit introduces \`read_file_region(path, offset, len, label)\`
which opens the file, seeks to the target offset, and reads exactly the
requested byte range. All metadata inspection paths now use this:

- \`dump_superblock_cmd\`: reads only the 1024-byte superblock region.
- \`dump_group_cmd\` / \`build_ext4_group_info\`: reads individual group
  descriptors (32 or 64 bytes each) via \`read_ext4_group_desc_from_path\`.
- \`dump_inode_cmd\`: reads the inode table entry via
  \`read_ext4_inode_from_path\`, which first reads the group descriptor
  to locate the inode table, then seeks to the inode offset.

The old \`checked_slice\` helper (which operated on an in-memory \`&[u8]\`)
is replaced by the new seek-based reader. The \`load_ext4_reader\` helper
is retained for paths that still need full-image access.

Additionally, the evidence ledger reader is refactored from loading the
entire file + parsing all lines into a streaming \`BufReader\`-based
approach. The new \`load_evidence_records\` function:
- Reads line-by-line, skipping malformed lines (torn writes).
- Applies event-type filtering inline to avoid allocating skipped records.
- Implements tail-mode via a bounded \`VecDeque\` ring buffer, keeping
  only the last N matching records in O(1) space.

A \`const fn evidence_event_type_name\` replaces the previous
serde_json round-trip for event type string matching.

Tests are added for:
- \`read_file_region\` in-bounds and out-of-bounds cases
- Evidence ledger filtering, tail mode, and malformed-line skipping
- Group descriptor, inode, and group-info reading from file paths

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
